Release Video rendered nothing for 27 consecutive releases

Two unrelated dependency bumps landed 24 hours apart and each broke the Remotion renderer. The first masked the second, and because the workflow only runs after a release is published, its red never gated anything. Nobody saw it for nine days.

27consecutive failed releases
9days undetected
2stacked root causes
0gates that could catch it

The blackout

v8.65.0 → v8.70.1  ·  13 rendered
v8.70.2 → v8.84.5  ·  27 failed
2026-07-05 break: 2026-07-13 → 07-14 2026-07-22

Two causes, stacked

1  ·  Remotion dropped the API the config called

remotion 4.0.484 → 4.0.489 Dependabot npm-minor-patch group, #2860, 07-13

Config.setExperimentalClientSideRenderingEnabled was removed upstream. remotion.config.ts called it at module load, so every render died before it began. There is no replacement: @remotion/cli@4.0.494 exposes 93 Config keys and none is a client-side-rendering flag.

2  ·  TypeScript 7 removed the JS API the bundler needs

typescript 6.0.3 → 7.0.2 #2869, 07-14

@remotion/bundler's esbuild-loader calls typescript.readConfigFile(tsConfigPath, typescript.sys.readFile). Under TS 7 both ts.sys and ts.readConfigFile are undefined. Remotion 4.x cannot build under TypeScript 7 at all.

Why this stayed hidden behind cause 1 
config load  →  TypeError: setExperimental… is not a function   ← CI only ever saw this
bundle       →  TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'readFile')

A root cause I recorded wrong, and corrected

First diagnosis (issue #3086)Actual
A floating ^4.0.494 caret range let an upstream release drift in. The workflow runs npm ci, which installs the lockfile exactly. Caret ranges were never consulted. The lockfile itself was bumped past the removal.

The fix

Drop the dead call, pin the tree to TypeScript 6

orchestkit-demos goes back to typescript ^6.0.3. The pin is contained: docs/site is already on ^6.0.3, no cross-tree TypeScript consistency gate exists, and nothing outside release-video.yml typechecks this tree.

Move the signal from post-release to pre-merge

New demos-validate.yml runs remotion compositions on any PR touching the demos tree. That loads the config and bundles with the TypeScript toolchain, so it would have failed the very Dependabot PRs that introduced both breakages. It also asserts the two compositions the release renders are registered.

Verified end to end, not inferred

CheckResult
npx remotion compositionsbundles; lists TPL-ReleaseNotes + TPL-ReleaseNotesSquare
npx remotion render TPL-ReleaseNotesreal 1.5 MB h264 MP4 produced
npm ci → render (final lockfile)typescript 6.0.3; 911 kB MP4 produced
actionlint demos-validate.ymlexit 0
composition assertionpasses positive and negative control

Closes #3086 · the negative control matters: it proves the new assertion fails when a composition is missing, rather than passing vacuously — the same failure class that let this hide for 27 releases.
